skill.name: pdf
skill.description: PDF toolkit for extracting text and tables, creating documents, merging or splitting files, and filling PDF forms.
skill.summary: Process PDF files, extract document data, create PDFs, and fill forms with guided Python workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found them to be false positives from Markdown examples, comments, and sample data. The only real concern is expected filesystem access for reading PDFs and writing user-selected outputs. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or malicious command execution.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Command examples appear in Markdown code blocks and inline instructions. They document standard PDF tools and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown examples for qpdf, pdftotext, pdfimages, and helper scripts. The Python scripts do not invoke shell commands.

low_findings.title: Expected Filesystem Output Writes
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: Helper scripts write JSON, image, and PDF outputs to paths supplied by the user. This is expected for PDF processing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write operations are direct and easy to verify. Risk is low because output paths are explicit CLI arguments for document processing.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Flagged as Network Activity
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Hardcoded URLs are license terms, PDF references, and issue links. No code sends document data to those URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in a license file and code comments only. No HTTP client, fetch call, or outbound request was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and C2 Keyword Matches Are Incidental
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: High-severity keyword matches occur in PDF examples, test data, and benign command text. No cryptographic logic or C2 behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain sample fields, file names, or PDF commands. They do not implement encryption, hashing, callbacks, or remote control.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Review Found No Malicious Override
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The skill contains workflow instructions for PDF forms. No text asks reviewers to skip analysis, lower risk, or ignore system instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The wording is imperative, but it stays within the PDF form workflow. It does not target the marketplace evaluator or security process.

content.value_statement: PDF work often requires fragile manual steps across forms, tables, images, and page operations. This skill provides repeatable workflows for extraction, creation, merging, splitting, and form filling.
actual_capabilities: Extract text and tables from PDFs with Python PDF libraries.
Fill interactive PDF form fields from mapped field values.
Add text annotations to static PDF forms using bounding boxes.
Merge, split, rotate, crop, watermark, and encrypt PDF files.
Create new PDFs with formatted text, pages, and tables.
Convert PDF pages to images for visual review and validation.
limitations: Requires Python PDF libraries and optional command-line tools to be installed.
Scanned documents need OCR tools before text extraction works well.
Static form filling depends on accurate visual bounding boxes.
It cannot safely recover unknown passwords for encrypted PDFs.

use_cases.title: Complete Application Forms
use_cases.description: Extract form fields, map applicant data, validate values, and produce completed PDFs.
use_cases.target_user: Operations teams that process repeated forms

use_cases.title: Extract Report Tables
use_cases.description: Read PDF reports, collect tables, and prepare structured data for spreadsheet review.
use_cases.target_user: Analysts working with PDF source documents

use_cases.title: Build Document Workflows
use_cases.description: Merge packets, split large files, create summaries, and generate new PDF outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Check a PDF Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether [file.pdf] has fillable fields. If it does, list each field name, type, and page.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: inspect a form before filling it.

prompt_templates.title: Fill a Known Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Fill [input.pdf] with values from [field_values.json]. Validate field IDs and write the completed file to [output.pdf].
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: complete an interactive PDF form.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Tables
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract every table from [report.pdf]. Preserve page numbers, headers, and row order, then create an Excel workbook.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: convert PDF tables into structured data.

prompt_templates.title: Fill a Static Form
prompt_templates.prompt: This PDF has no fillable fields. Create page images, identify entry boxes, validate bounding boxes, and add text annotations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: complete a flat or scanned form.

output_examples.input: Check whether application.pdf has fillable fields.
output: The PDF has fillable form fields.
Found text fields for name, address, and email.
Found checkbox fields for consent and eligibility.
Saved the field summary for value mapping.
Preserved detected headers and row order.
Created a workbook with one sheet per table.
Flagged two rows for manual review.
Converted pages to images for visual placement.
Created validation images with entry and label boxes.
No bounding box intersections were found.
Added text annotations and wrote the completed PDF.

best_practices: Work on copies of source PDFs before writing final outputs.
Validate form fields and bounding boxes before generating completed files.
Use explicit input and output paths for every PDF operation.
anti_patterns: Do not skip visual validation for static or scanned forms.
Do not assume extracted table headers are correct without review.
Do not overwrite original PDFs when testing a workflow.

faq.question: What PDF tasks does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports extraction, form filling, annotation placement, merging, splitting, rotation, watermarking, encryption, and PDF creation.

faq.question: Can it fill scanned or flat forms?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses page images, bounding boxes, validation images, and text annotations for forms without interactive fields.

faq.question: Does it require external software?
faq.answer: Some workflows need Python libraries. OCR and command-line workflows may require pytesseract, pdf2image, poppler-utils, or qpdf.

faq.question: Can it extract tables from PDF reports?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use pdfplumber examples to extract tables and prepare spreadsheet-ready data.

faq.question: How are checkboxes handled?
faq.answer: For fillable forms, it extracts valid checked and unchecked values. For static forms, it places an X annotation.

faq.question: Is this safe for sensitive documents?
faq.answer: The scripts operate on local files. Users should still handle sensitive PDFs in a trusted environment.
